name: ABDO عبدو
location: Gouraud Street, Bourj Hammoud, Mount Lebanon Governorate, Lebanon
all_reviews: [4.0] [8 years ago on Zomato]: Absolutely love this place. I stumbled upon this place on a hungry morning and decided to try their Egg wrap which was really delicious. Next came the succulent Halloumi Bacon wrap which was also a delicious juicy combination. I also sat for some of their plates especially their beans (foul) which came with the perfect sides of fresh veggies and hot freshly baked bread. The decor and music are also a great match with the experience, especially the lighting. :D Fair prices for the quality of the food. Recommended || [5.0] [8 years ago on Zomato]: The food is amazing here, we tried the kaake chicken burger and we loved it and we loved the potato with the dipping sauces also ! Great decoration and friendly staff. A must try restaurant! || [5.0] [8 years ago on Zomato]: We have personally known Abdo since forever and we were delighted to learn that his long studied dough has finally a place to shine! The passion behind the place is clear, Abdo restaurant is composed of a team of young men including M3alim Abdo, passionate about their food and who have developed their special dough into a unique piece of art that cannot be found anywhere else in Lebanon. It may seem as your local bakery shop, but its not, the taste of Saj, Mne2ich and Kaak you get at this place beats any you ever had before. The dough is prepared through a complex and long process and is given the right time to rest, ferment and become the delicious light product it is. We know firsthand that this is the result of long research hours and experiments done by Abdo himself for years. The quality of food is excellent yet at very fair prices. The ambiance is very homey and cozy. We recommend their Zaatar Baladi, simply delicious and light and really Baladi! We absolutely love their Sojok and Cheese and Beid bi Awerma, AMAZING! Halloum & Pesto Kaake is also recommended, the Kaake has a crunchy yet thin texture that you will love. Their Ayran with Thyme is really something, made of the simplest ingredients but worth every sip. This is a place developed with love, serving the best premium Lebanese products and we guarantee you will not regret your visit. Abdo, we are coming back soon! ;) || [4.0] [8 years ago on Zomato]: This was our first time at Abdo. I came here because I’ve heard so much about this place. After trying out the food thats ultimately amazing! The dough is great! I’ve asked the manager who’s very friendly how long had they been in the market and he told me 3 months in Hamra and 1 month in Gemmayze. They deserve a clap for bringing us great food and atmosphere in such a short time. I’m definitely coming back, the beid bi awarma was really good along with the pesto chicken. Thumbs up guys! || [5.0] [8 years ago on Zomato]: We were welcomed by Mr. Ghassan and M3alim Abdo to their Mar Mkhayel parlor! The place has 3 tables which seat 4 people each. The colors are soothing and simple; a lot of white, a bit of black and red! Mr. Abdo the honor is a vey humble man.  He was a history teacher who found his passion in making dough - in his free time; all kinds of dough. Let me assure you, he's an expert in dough! He uses no sugar, and lets the dough rest for 30 hrs making it lose 70% of the carbs. Lemonade: We had their fresh lemonade with ginger as we chatted. It was very refreshing and tasty no bitter or artificial taste whatsoever.  Then Mr. or M3alim Abdo suggested a few items to order. For starters we had: Zaatar Akhdar/Zoubaa'i  Freshly grinned thyme spread on a breathtaking dough with tomato and fresh mints; served with fresh veggies and labneh. Very enjoyable and light. Jebne and Sejok  Juicy sejok and melted cheese spread on that amazing dough served with tasty pickles. An orgasmic choice full of flavors. Next we ordered our mains: Pesto Chicken Wrap  Homemade pesto sauce so dear to my heart. The chicken was cooked to perfection and that dough was beyond amazing! A great combination! Burger Kaake  A kaake filled with a burger patty, sautéed fresh mushrooms and cheese! Very juicy, very tender and well balanced. It was amazing! The fries were perfectly fried and sprinkled with thyme. Club Kaake  Eggs, tomatoes, pickles, chicken and lettuce very fulfilling with a simple mustard sauce all stuffed into a small kaake. What's for dessert? Ka2ana Knefe  This is knefe goals! I'm in love! Stretchy cheese, crunchy osmalieh crust and just the right amount of qater and crushed pistachios! A must try !! Apple Crumble:  Served cold, this baby has apples seared, crumbles, pistachios and raisins. A more oriental version of the hot apple crumble served worldwide. M3alim Abdo you're a dough genius! We really enjoyed our dinner it was light yet fulfilling; filled with explosive flavors and a guilt free feel! We will be coming back soon; again and again! Highly recommended || [4.5] [8 years ago on Zomato]: Best thing about this place is that you can enjoy a dough-full breakfast without feeling "doughy" afterwards. Best dough I've tried in Lebanon so far !  Would like to thank abdo for the invitation and for going through the whole process of dough making with us. The food was great we tried the sujuk and jebneh saj , chicken pest wrap , zaatar akhdar saj, burger ka3ke (wow) , almost knefeh (amazing) .. Everything tasted unique ! Keep it up !! || [5.0] [8 years ago on Zomato]: I want to begin by first thanking Abdo team for inviting me to try their delicious food, and it was interesting to know about simple yet effective details about the dough from Abdo himself. Concept: Abdo is a former History teacher who, with time, discovered a passion for baking that resides deep inside of him. He then decided to pursue this passion by creating his very own “aged” dough, which is sugar-free, relying on the natural fermentation process of the yeast. Abdo’s dough needs around 24 hours to mature unlike the commercial dough which contains sugar to enhance the yeast’s feeding. Abdo stopped teaching history but history has never completely disappeared from his profession. Abdo processes a cozy venue with a simplicity that intrigues you first thing when you step into the restaurant. A chock board that lists the menu items and a counter behind which your food gets prepared on display, a couple of tables around which wooden chairs are placed, and the most basic and traditional tableware constituted of metallic dishes, glass jars for cups, metallic pans for plates, and guess what, their baskets are covered with papers that contain exerts from the history textbook. The whole thing tells a story on the behalf of M3allem Abdo which you’re lucky enough if you get to listen to its details from Abdo himself. Food: When science meets food, something like Abdo’s dough is created: a perfectly light bread that is easy to digest. This kind of bread is crafted with passion to meet high standards of production. I was surprised by the great taste of beef patty in a Kaake. That was something indescribable. There’s even a secret to the sauce which does not taste like your every day burger sauce but is still 100% Lebanese and homemade. The taste of the beef patty isn’t disguised in a sugar-loaded bun but rather in a light Kaake to make a magical burger! Thanks m3allem! I also tasted the Chicken Pesto wrap and this too was super special. It’s done with genuine Pesto sauce that is prepared in house. I couldn’t get enough of it! Also, don’t miss their sour Wild Zaatar saj and don’t miss the side Labne dip which is a mix of cow and goat Labne. Moving to desserts, Abdo’s creations are amazing! كأنها كنافة is going to become your next addiction. Even if you’re not a great knefe fan, you’re going to discover a new love: filled up with luscious cheese and covered with othmalieh. Ending up with it is a must! Don’t leave without trying it. Last but not least, if you’re craving a sweet indulgence, the Apple with Caramel is one great choice: a yummy mess of apples, caramel, cinnamon, and crumbs in a pot. Abdo restaurant is where you’d certainly feel at home; particularly in a house in the village where your grandparents reside. It’s so full of love and smiles. Recommended for breakfast, lunch, and a late night light dinner. I would like to thank the manager for his welcoming attitude, hospitality, good company, light conversation, and the smile that accompanied us from the moment we entered to the time we left. Thanks to M3allem Abdo for giving me the time for a small chat full of wisdom and genuineness. With love, Nour :) || [4.0] [8 years ago on Zomato]: The dough is reallyyyy good!
